bid: verb. To issue a command; to tell.
bin: noun. A box, frame, crib, or enclosed place, used as a storage container.
bind: verb. To tie; to confine by any ligature.
bindi: noun. The “holy dot” traditionally worn on the forehead of Hindu women.
blin: verb. To cease (from); to stop; to desist, to let up.
blind: adjective. Unable to see, or only partially able to see.
blini: noun. A small pancake, of Russian origin, made from buckwheat flour; traditionally served with melted butter, sour cream and caviar or
bluing: noun. A substance used in laundry to whiten clothes.
bud: noun. A newly sprouted leaf or blossom that has not yet unfolded.
bug: noun. An insect of the order Hemiptera (the “true bugs”).
build: verb. To form (something) by combining materials or parts.
building: noun. The act or process by which something is built; construction.
bun: noun. Senses referring to baked goods.
bund: noun. A league or confederacy; especially the confederation of German states.
bung: noun. A stopper, alternative to a cork, often made of rubber, used to prevent fluid passing through the neck of a bottle, vat, a hole in a
dib: verb. To dig a hole by poking; especially, to dig a small hole in soil for the purpose of planting a bulb or seed.
dig: verb. To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
dub: verb. To confer knighthood; the conclusion of the ceremony was marked by a tap on the shoulder with a sword, the accolade.
dug: verb. simple past and past participle of dig (replacing earlier digged)
gib: noun. A castrated male cat or ferret.
gid: noun. A disease caused by coenurosis of the brain, most commonly found in sheep and canids.
gild: verb. To cover with a thin layer of gold; to cover with gold leaf.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
glib: adjective. Having a ready flow of words but lacking thought or understanding; superficial; shallow.
gnu: noun. A large antelope of the genus Connochaetes, native to Africa, having curved horns.
guild: noun. A group or association mainly of tradespeople made up of merchants, craftspeople, or artisans, particularly in the Middle Ages,
gul: noun. Any medallionlike design on a Central or West Asian rug.
gun: noun. A device for shooting projectiles or ditto (a shooter), functioning through stored energy: a firearm, cannon, harpoon gun / spear
idling: verb. present participle and gerund of idle
lid: noun. The top or cover of a container.
lin: verb. To desist, to stop, to cease.
ling: noun. Any of various codlike saltwater fish often used as food by humans, those of the genus Molva, in the family Lotidae.
lug: noun. The act of hauling or dragging.
lung: noun. A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.
lungi: noun. A garment worn around the waist, especially by men, in Southern India, Bangladesh, Burma, and Pakistan.
nib: noun. The tip of a pen or tool that touches the surface, transferring ink to paper.
nub: noun. The innermost section of a chrysalis in a silk cocoon.
bld: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Bolango.
bling: noun. An ostentatious display of richness or style.
budling: noun. A small, young, or immature bud.
glid: verb. simple past and past participle of glide
glub: noun. The sound of underwater bubbles, or of water bubbling (often used repetitively).
guib: noun. The harnessed antelope (Tragelaphus scriptus), a West African antelope marked with white stripes and spots on a reddish fawn ground;.
gundi: noun. Any of the several North African rodents of the family Ctenodactylidae.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
lig: verb. To lie; be in a prostrate or recumbent position.
lind: noun. The lime tree, or linden tree.
lubing: verb. present participle and gerund of lube
lud: noun. Pronunciation spelling of lord (used for addressing a judge).
nbg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nagarchal.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nul: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nusa Laut.
ugli: noun. Citrus × paradisi, a cross between a tangerine, Citrus reticulata, and a grapefruit Citrus paradisi, grown in the West Indies.
undig: verb. To undo the process of digging; to fill up (a hole or grave) or bury again (something unearthed).
unlid: verb. To remove the lid from.
